metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state via TypeORM (supporting SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB) with 30 entities including ChatFlow, Credential, ApiKey, and ChatMessage. It exposes full CRUD operations on these entities through a comprehensive REST API.
metadata.context: Low-code UI for building LLM flows and agents.

findings.title: Machine Identity Authentication — No Principal Attribution
findings.description: The application supports API key authentication with workspace-scoped keys and JWT authentication via Passport.js. However, API keys are not attributed to a specific machine principal — they are generic per-workspace keys without agent identity differentiation. The system cannot distinguish which agent made a call in audit logs.
findings.gap: No machine identity with principal attribution for API-key-authenticated requests. API keys identify a workspace, not an agent principal. Audit logs do not record which API key was used for data operations.
findings.recommendation: Extend the ApiKey entity to include a principalName or agentIdentity field. Log the API key ID alongside every write operation in a structured audit trail.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Extend the ApiKey entity to include a principalName or agentIdentity field. Log the API key ID alongside every write operation.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Every API request authenticated via API key is attributed to a named principal in immutable audit logs. Multiple agent identities can be distinguished within a workspace.

findings.title: Scoped Permissions — Non-Enterprise Keys Lack Granularity
findings.description: The ApiKey entity has a permissions field (JSON array), and the enterprise RBAC system defines 60+ granular permissions across 15 categories. However, non-enterprise API keys do not enforce granular permission scoping — a key grants access to all chatflow operations within its workspace.
findings.gap: Non-enterprise API keys lack granular permission scoping. An agent API key cannot be restricted to read-only access on specific chatflows.
findings.recommendation: Extend API key permission enforcement to non-enterprise tier. Allow API keys to be scoped to specific operations.

compensating_controls: Create workspace-per-agent to limit blast radius (workspace isolation is enforced)
Use enterprise RBAC features to create agent-specific roles with minimal permissions

findings.title: Immutable Audit Logging — Login Events Only
findings.description: The audit logging system (enterprise-only LoginActivity entity) records only login events (success/failure, username, login mode). It does not record CRUD operations on resources, does not log the authenticated principal for data operations, and is not immutable.
findings.gap: No audit trail for data operations (only login events). No immutable storage. No principal attribution for API-key-authenticated requests in audit logs.
findings.recommendation: Implement operation-level audit logging that records the authenticated principal, action performed, resource affected, and timestamp. Store in an append-only destination.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, but audit logging is still important for system maturity and forensics.
compensating_controls: Enable request logging middleware with API key identity to a separate log destination
Configure external CloudTrail or CloudWatch Logs with retention policies

findings.title: Agent Identity Suspension — Delete Only, No Suspend
findings.description: API keys can be deleted via the API (DELETE /api/v1/apikey/{id}), which effectively revokes access. However, there is no suspend or disable mechanism — revocation is permanent deletion. No automated anomaly-based suspension exists.
findings.gap: No suspend/disable mechanism for API keys (only permanent deletion). No automated anomaly detection to trigger suspension.
findings.recommendation: Add an isActive boolean to the ApiKey entity with middleware enforcement. This allows keys to be suspended without deletion and re-enabled after investigation.

compensating_controls: Use API Gateway in front of Flowise with per-key usage plans that can be disabled
Monitor API key usage via request logs and manually delete keys exhibiting anomalous behavior

findings.title: Rate Limiting — Prediction Endpoints Only
findings.description: Per-chatflow rate limiting is implemented via express-rate-limit with Redis-backed distributed store. However, rate limiting is only applied to prediction endpoints — administrative API endpoints (CRUD on chatflows, credentials, tools) have no rate limiting.
findings.gap: Rate limiting covers prediction endpoints only. Administrative APIs are unprotected against traffic storms.
findings.recommendation: Add rate limiting middleware to all /api/v1/ routes. Configure aggressive limits for write operations and moderate limits for read operations.

compensating_controls: Deploy behind an API Gateway with global throttling on all endpoints
Configure WAF rate rules at the infrastructure level

findings.title: PII Redaction in Logs — Opt-In Only
findings.description: The application has configurable log sanitization via LOG_SANITIZE_BODY_FIELDS and LOG_SANITIZE_HEADER_FIELDS environment variables. However, this sanitization is opt-in (requires configuration), and chat messages containing user PII are stored without redaction.
findings.gap: Log sanitization is opt-in, not default. Chat message content (potentially containing PII) is logged and stored without redaction. No automated PII detection.
findings.recommendation: Enable PII sanitization by default (not opt-in). Add automated PII detection for chat message content before logging.

compensating_controls: Configure LOG_SANITIZE_BODY_FIELDS with comprehensive field list
Deploy Amazon Macie on S3-stored logs to detect PII leakage

findings.title: Machine-Readable API Spec — Manually Maintained
findings.description: A comprehensive Swagger/OpenAPI specification exists (2,664 lines) covering major endpoints. It documents parameters, request bodies, and response schemas. However, it appears manually maintained and may drift from implementation.
findings.gap: Manually maintained spec may drift from implementation. Enterprise endpoints not fully documented.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec validation to CI. Consider swagger-jsdoc for auto-generation.

compensating_controls: Use the existing swagger spec for agent tool generation
Add spec validation to CI pipeline to detect drift

findings.title: Structured Error Responses — No Error Codes
findings.description: Centralized errorHandlerMiddleware returns { statusCode, success: false, message, stack }. No machine-readable error code enum or retryable field exists.
findings.gap: No machine-readable error codes. No retryable indicator. Agents cannot distinguish retriable vs terminal errors programmatically.
findings.recommendation: Add an errorCode field (enum) and retryable boolean to error responses.

compensating_controls: Agents can infer retryability from HTTP status codes (429, 503 = retry; 400, 401, 403 = terminal)
Wrap Flowise API with an adapter layer that adds structured error codes

findings.title: Distributed Tracing — Metrics Only, No Traces
findings.description: OpenTelemetry implementation covers metrics only (counters and histograms). Tracing is commented out. Winston logging outputs JSON but without correlation IDs or trace IDs.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing. No trace/correlation ID in structured logs. Cannot reconstruct agent-initiated request flows.
findings.recommendation: Enable OpenTelemetry tracing provider. Add traceparent header propagation. Include traceId in all log entries.

compensating_controls: Use HTTP request logging with timestamps to approximate request tracing
Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector sidecar to add trace context at the infrastructure layer

findings.title: Alerting — Metrics Collected But No Alerts Configured
findings.description: Prometheus and OpenTelemetry metrics are collected. Grafana dashboards exist. However, no alerting rules or thresholds are configured.
findings.gap: No alerting thresholds. No alert routing. API degradation would go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Define alerting rules for error rate, P99 latency, and rate limit exhaustion.

compensating_controls: Deploy Prometheus AlertManager with rules on exported metrics
Configure CloudWatch alarms on OTEL-exported metrics

findings.title: Schema Versioning — No Breaking Change Detection
findings.description: API is URL-versioned (/api/v1/). Database schema managed via TypeORM migrations. However, no automated breaking change detection in CI, no contract testing.
findings.gap: No automated breaking change detection. No consumer-driven contract tests. No formal API changelog.
findings.recommendation: Add oasdiff or similar tool to CI. Publish API changelog per release.

compensating_controls: URL-versioned API provides stable base for agent tool bindings
TypeORM migrations ensure database schema evolution is tracked

findings.title: Infrastructure Governance — No IaC
findings.description: The repository contains Docker definitions and GitHub Actions for CI/CD, but has NO Infrastructure as Code. Infrastructure provisioning is not codified.
findings.gap: No IaC for agent-facing infrastructure surface. No drift detection. Infrastructure changes not subject to peer review.
findings.recommendation: Define deployment infrastructure as Terraform or CDK. Enable AWS Config drift detection.

compensating_controls: Docker Compose definitions provide reproducible deployment configuration
AWS ECR workflow defines the build-and-push pipeline

findings.title: CI/CD — No API Contract Testing
findings.description: CI pipeline includes linting, building, unit tests, and E2E tests. No API contract testing — no Pact, no OpenAPI validation, no breaking change detection.
findings.gap: No API contract testing in CI. No automated detection of breaking API changes.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec validation step to CI. Add oasdiff for breaking change detection on PRs.

compensating_controls: E2E Cypress tests cover some API behavior
Manual review of API changes during PR review

findings.title: Rollback Capability — Manual Only
findings.description: Docker images are tagged and pushed to ECR with version tags. However, no automated rollback mechanism exists. Rollback requires manually changing the image tag.
findings.gap: No automated rollback. No blue/green or canary deployment. No health-check-triggered rollback.
findings.recommendation: Deploy via ECS/EKS with rolling deployment and automatic rollback on health check failure.

compensating_controls: Docker image versioning allows manual rollback by redeploying previous tag
Health check endpoint can detect failures
